content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Our features both touch InternationalizationExtension.parse()/_make_node() in the same file, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible here. Given we each operate in our own sandboxed /workspace/repo and submit independent patch.txt files, there's no real clobber risk to worry about. For cleanliness I'll keep my domain-parameter changes localized: (1) in parse()'s param while-loop, a new elif branch for token.value == 'domain' parsing a string literal, raising TemplateAssertionError on duplicate/non-string; (2) pass domain through to _make_node's signature/call; (3) in _make_node, branch to dgettext/dngettext (via new _make_new_dgettext-style helpers or direct nodes.Call to 'dgettext'/'dngettext' globals) when domain is set, else fall back to gettext/ngettext; (4) register dgettext/dngettext in _install_callables/_install/_install_null globals. Proceeding to implement now — good luck with trans_metadata!
